solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def combine(self, n: int, k: int) -> List[List[int]]:
        def backtrack(start, path):
            if len(path) == k:
                res.append(path)
                return
            for i in range(start, n + 1):
                backtrack(i + 1, path + [i])
        res = []
        backtrack(1, [])
        return res
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n choose k)
space complexity: O(n choose k)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses backtracking to generate all combinations of k numbers chosen from the range [1, n]. The backtrack function recursively adds numbers to the current path until the path has k numbers, at which point it is added to the result list. The function starts at 1 and iterates up to n, ensuring that each number is only used once in each combination.
